Administrator: USDA, Rural Development Housing and Community Facilities Program
Purpose: Enable low- and moderate-income rural residents to purchase modestly priced homes
Mechanism: Mortgage Loan from commercial lender guaranteed 90% by the government with 30 year terms and fixed market-level interest rates

Administrator: USDA, Rural Development Housing and Community Facilities Program
Purpose: Enable very low- and low-income rural residents to purchase, build, repair, renovate, relocate modest homes and sites
Mechanism: Mortgage Loan for up to 33 years, no down payment, combined payment for principal, interest, taxes, and insurance higher of either 24% of annual income or 1% principal/interest plus taxes/insurance

Program Title: Rural Repair and Rehabilitation Loans and Grants
Administrator: USDA, Rural Development Housing and Community Facilities Program
Purpose: Finance repairs for very low income homeowners
Mechanism: Loan for general repairs and improvements or remove health/safety hazards for very low income; Grant to remove health/safety hazards or make useable/accessible to disabled for low income elderly

Administrator: USDA, Rural Development Housing and Community Facilities Program
Purpose: Finance affordable rental housing for very low-, low-, and moderate-income families, elderly, and disabled
Mechanism: Loan for up to 30 years at 1% interest rate, amortized over 50 years; tenants pay basic rent or 30% of adjusted income
Other Aspects: For targeted communities (state-level)

Program Title: Farm Labor Housing Loans and Grants
Administrator: USDA, Rural Development Housing and Community Facilities Program
Purpose: Develop on- and off-farm housing
Mechanism: Loan (514) to buy, build, improve (incl. durable furnishings), or repair housing, day care, and community facilities at 1% interest for 33 years; Grant (516) of up to 90% of costs for the same

Administrator: USDA, Rural Development Housing and Community Facilities Program
Purpose: Finance affordable rental housing for very low- and low-income families, elderly, and disabled
Mechanism: Subsidy to limit tenants' payments to 30% of their income; for some section 515 and section 514/516 properties
Other Aspects: ?for targeted communities (state-level)

Administrator: USDA, Rural Development Housing and Community Facilities Program
Purpose: Finance construction of owner-built homes for very low- and low-income households
Mechanism: Loan for up to 33 years, no down payment, combined payment for principal, interest, taxes, and insurance higher of either 24% of annual income or 1% principal/interest plus taxes/insurance

Administrator: USDA, Rural Development Housing and Community Facilities Program
Purpose: Enable the acquisition and development of site for homes of low- and moderate-income families
Mechanism: Loan to nonprofit organizations for 2 years at market interest rates

Administrator: USDA, Rural Development Housing and Community Facilities Program
Purpose: Fund the repair and rehabilitation of very low- and low-income housing
Mechanism: Grant is made in area of concentrated need and must be used within two years

Administrator: USDA, Rural Development Housing and Community Facilities Program
Purpose: Enable the development of affordable multifamily rural rental housing for tenants of moderate-income or below
Mechanism: Loan from commercial lender guaranteed 90% by the government with up to 40 year terms and fixed interest rates; tenants pay basic rent or 30% of adjusted income

Law: Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act, Section 306
Administrator: USDA, Rural Development Housing and Community Facilities Program
Purpose: Fund clean and reliable drinking water systems, sanitary sewage disposal, sanitary solid waste disposal, and storm water drainage
Mechanism: Loan(sometimes combined with Grant) for up to 40 years at fixed interest rate depending on need and median household income

Law: Housing Act of 1959, Section 202; amended by NAHA 1990, Section 801
Purpose: Provide funding for construction, rehabilitation, and operation of housing for elderly, very low-income households
Mechanism: Capital Advance Loan (no-interest loan) for up to 40 years; Project Rental Assistance Contracts; tenants pay basic rent or 30% of adjusted income
Other Aspects: Not specifically for rural areas

Program Title: Supportive Housing Program for Persons with Disabilities
Law: National Affordable Housing Act of 1990, Section 811
Purpose: Provide funding for construction or rehabilitation to developers of housing for disabled, very low-income households
Mechanism: Capital Advance Loan (no-interest loan) for up to 40 years; tenants pay basic rent or 30% of adjusted income
Other Aspects: Not specifically for rural areas

Program Title: Rural Capacity Building for Community Development and Affordable Housing Grants
Purpose: Provide training/education, financial and technical assistance to housing  development organizations, corporations, and local governments for low- and moderate-income households
Mechanism: Grant

Purpose: Finance decent, safe, and sanitary housing for very low-income, elderly, and disabled
Mechanism: Subsidy to limit tenants' payments to 30% of their income; voucher pays the difference between the limit and a local payment standard
Other Aspects: Not specifically for rural areas

Law: Housing and Economic Recovery Act of 2008, Section 1131
Purpose: Fund the production or preservation of affordable housing for extremely low- and very low-income and homeless families
Mechanism: Grant to states used for a variety of forms of assistance where at least 80% is used for rental housing, up to 10% for homeownership, and up to 10% for administrative costs
Other Aspects: Not specifically for rural areas

Mechanism: Tax Deduction

Purpose: Promote acquisition, rehabilitation, and construction of low-income rental housing
Mechanism: Tax Creditor up to 10 years
Other Aspects: Not specifically for rural areas

Law: Community Renewal Tax Relief Act of 2000, renewed  most recently by Protecting American from Tax Hiked Act of 2015
Administrator: US Department of Treasury, Community Development Financial Institutions Fund
Purpose: Incentivize community development and economic growth in distressed communities
Mechanism: Tax Credit for 39% of original investment amount over 7 years on federal income tax
Other Aspects: Not specifically for rural areas; Non-permanent program that must be renewed periodically

Program Title: Mortgage Insurance for Rental and Cooperative Housing
Purpose: Facilitate construction or rehabilitation of multifamily or cooperative housing for moderate-income families, elderly, and disabled
Mechanism: Loan for up to 40 years guaranteed by the government for between 83.3% and 90% of property value, depending on degree of low-income assistance
Other Aspects: Not specifically for rural areas; for market rate, affordable, and subsidized housing

Program Title: Mortgage Insurance for Purchase or Refinancing of Existing Multifamily Rental Housing
Purpose: Facilitate purchase or refinance of multifamily rental housing
Mechanism: Loan for up to 35 years guaranteed by the government for between 83.3% and 90% of property value, depending on degree of low-income assistance
Other Aspects: Not specifically for rural areas; for market rate, affordable, and subsidized housing

Purpose: Finance purchase or renovation of owner-occupied housing in rural small communities
Mechanism: Loan at Rural Program interest rate for first $250,000 and at blended interest rate of additional 1% thereafter
Other Aspects: Other options that are not rural-specific: Affordable Housing Enhanced Loan, Energy Efficiency Interest Rate Reduction, Low-Income Interest Rate Reduction, State Veterans Preference; Also rental assistance programs for low-income, elderly, veterans, and displaced families

Purpose: Alleviate deficiencies in the supply of safe, affordable, accessible housing for very low-income families
Mechanism: Loan or Grant to assist in new construction/ rehabilitation/rental assistance/land acquisition/ predevelopment costs/infrastructure/transitional housing/down payment assistance/housing and foreclosure counseling/technical assistance
Other Aspects: Not specifically for rural areas, but specifically mentioned in mission

Mechanism: Counseling about rental and utility assistance/reverse mortgage/mortgage default/ delinquency/credit and debt counseling
Other Aspects: Not specifically for rural areas, but specifically mentioned in locations

Purpose: Develop affordable housing in rural and suburban areas
Mechanism: No specific mechanism mentioned
Other Aspects: Funded by CHFA but administered by Housing Connections

Purpose: Improve overall community development; housing one of the methods mentioned
Mechanism: No specific mechanism mentioned

Purpose: Finance affordable city or county rental housing in locations with a housing shortage
Mechanism: Loan for up to 50% of the total project development budget up to $1 million or $50,000 per unit for up to 20 years
Other Aspects: Not specifically for rural areas; preference for cities with fewer than 50,000 residents

Purpose: Promote construction or rehabilitation of housing in greyfield (former commercial), brownfield (former industrial), or greenfield (workforce housing needs) sites
Mechanism: Tax Refund for sales, service, and use taxes paid to the state during construction; Investment Tax Credit of up to 10% of new investment, up to $150,000 for each home/unit
Other Aspects: Not specifically for rural areas

Purpose: Assist development of rural housing through financing public improvements
Mechanism: Tax Funding from 100% of the incremental increase in real property taxes for up to 15 years
Other Aspects: For cities with 40,000 or fewer residents in a county with a population ≤ 60,000 or counties with 40,000 or fewer residents

Program Title: Moderate Income Housing Loans and Grants
Purpose: Develop new infrastructure and housing in rural areas for moderate-income families that do not qualify for federal housing assistance
Mechanism: Loan or Grant to communities
Other Aspects: For communities with 60,000 or fewer residents

Program Title: Minnesota Urban and Rural Homesteading Program
Purpose: Stop and prevent blight through financing the acquisition and rehabilitation of dilapidated single family housing
Mechanism: Loan from seller with no interest
Other Aspects: "Homes are sold to at-risk borrowers, who agree to follow a 'good neighbor' policy, via an interest-free contract for deed."; DEFUNCT

Program Title: Local Option Municipal Economic Development Act
Purpose: Promote economic development in incorporated places
Mechanism: Direct or indirect financial assistance raised from local taxes (via Loans, Grants, Guarantees, and Bonds) to qualified businesses
Other Aspects: Cities with more than 10,000 residents (except Omaha and Lincoln) can use these funds for housing

Program Title: "Home at Last" Down Payment Assistance Grants
Mechanism: Grant for between 2% and 5% of loan amount to cover down payment and/or closing costs paired with a loan that has a fixed interest rate for up to 30 years
Other Aspects: For communities with 150,000 or fewer residents

Mechanism: Tax credit for up to 20% of annual interest paid on loan on federal taxes
Other Aspects: For communities with 150,000 or fewer residents; First time homebuyers

Purpose: Ensure rural homebuyers have access to information and assistance
Mechanism: Counseling about financing programs and the home buying process
Other Aspects: One relator and one lender per rural region

Purpose: Provide housing assistance through property rehabilitation, counseling, mediation, legal assistance, employment programs, and community development
Mechanism: Financial Support to non-profit community-based housing corporations
Other Aspects: A sister program exists that does similar work in urban neighborhoods

Purpose: Provide communities with financial and technical assistance for the improvement of housing, commercial areas, and community facilities
Mechanism: No specific mechanism mentioned

Purpose: Provide affordable multifamily rental housing for low-income elderly and families through a financial support to housing companies
Mechanism: Subsidy to limit tenants' payments to 30% of their income; for section 515 properties for up to 25 years
Other Aspects: Rural - population less than 10,000, or less than 25,000 in areas with a lack of mortgage credit; In conjunction with federal programs

Purpose: Enable very low- and low-income, rural homebuyers to purchase a home
Mechanism: Mortgage Loan for 30 years at 0% interest for a first mortgage and 1% interest for a second mortgage
Other Aspects: Combination of federal HOME program funds and USDA Section 502 direct loans; USDA definition of rural and low-income; DEFUNCT (no longer mentioned on website)

Administrator: North Dakota Housing Finance Agency, Planning and Housing Development Division
Purpose: Finance predevelopment soft costs, land acquisition, site development, and other costs to produce housing
Mechanism: Loan to communities or non-profits for up to 75% of total cost with low interest rates for up to 3 years
Other Aspects: For rural communities

Administrator: North Dakota Housing Finance Agency, Planning and Housing Development Division
Purpose: Finance rehabilitation of single- or multi-family properties to decent, safe, and sanitary condition for residents at or below median income
Mechanism: Revolving Loan for up to 75% of total costs up to $25,000 for single- and $100,000 for multi-family over up to 20 and 25 years, respectively
Other Aspects: For rural communities

Administrator: North Dakota Housing Finance Agency, Planning and Housing Development Division
Purpose: Provide funding for analysis of current and future community housing needs
Mechanism: Grant for 50% of market survey, up to $5,000
Other Aspects: For rural communities

Administrator: North Dakota Housing Finance Agency, Planning and Housing Development Division
Purpose: Enable the construction or rehabilitation of affordable single-family housing
Mechanism: Loan guaranteed by the state government for the less of 50% of net loss or $93,500 for 18 months after closing
Other Aspects: For rural communities at least 10 miles from urban communities

Purpose: Develop and preserve farmworker housing
Mechanism: Loan for 35% of project costs up to $100,000 for up to 10 years with a 1% interest rate for non-profits and a 3% interest rate for for-profits

Administrator: Office of Rural and Farmworker Housing
Purpose: Finance the move from conceptual stages to actual plans for community development
Mechanism: Loan for early development costs at low interest rates
